violations.description: Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Sani Bucket used at blizzard blenders and server area is also used for public table tops/surfaces.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Gravy portions in walk in cooler are not date marked (stated as made yesterday). -Hot dogs in walk in cooler without date mark (stated as opened today).

General equipment and utensils - Use limitations of certain materials. — --Material of food contact equipment/utensil is not approved for use by food service code. -Onion rings stored in small black trash can (without liner) at upright freezer-could not verify container as a food safe material.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — ---Push button at interior of walk in cooler door is missing. *Have to leave door open to avoid being locked inside.--Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. -Gasket at upright freezer is split through. -Rough/worn cabinet below drive through soda dispenser.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface used with potentially hazardous food was not cleaned every four hours while in use or as frequently as required. - ~3pm observed tomato slicer had left dirty after use, food debris dried to blades and surfaces. Staff stated today's first use at ~10a.m. -Tongs at burgers with residue build up. -Multiple items stated as rinsed throughout day. ***Ensure full wash, rinse and sanitize of food containers and untensils within 4 hours of food contact if left at room temperature.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Commercial can opener with debris at blade and surround. -Microwave with splatter at upper interior. -Debris in bottom of big ice bucket with residue at side edges of both ice buckets.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Some dust/residue at bottom shelf of rolling storage rack (buns storage). -Dust/debris at floor fans and at walk in freezer fan covers. -Debris in bottom of ice scoop tray used at drive through soda machine. -Residue on recipe cards.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -Case of lids on floor across from mop sink. -Case of cups on floor in dry storage room.--Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. -Trash can next to dessert prep table-bottom shelf used to stored exposed single use cake trays.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room was not provided with a tight fitting or self closing door. -Men's restroom door does not self-close.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -Ceiling tiles throughout kitchen are stained and/or acoustic ceiling tiles. -Door transition around walk in freezer is unfinished with exposed insulation. -No outlet cover at outlet behind kitchen storage shelf.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Dirty light switch cover at dry storage room. -Dust/debris at bathroom ceiling fans.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. -Jackets and personal items hanging on soda bib rack. -Personal drink without lid stored above dessert topping storage. Personal food/consumption at back prep table near cinnamon toast dip.--Lockers or other suitable facilities were not provided for the orderly storage of employees' clothing/possessions.
Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Sani Bucket used at blizzard blenders and server area is also used for wiping cloths used on public table tops/surfaces.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Hot dogs in make table and walk in cooler are not date marked. -Gravy portions are not date marked.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. -Gasket at upright freezer is split through. -Rough/worn cabinet below drive through soda dispenser.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface used with potentially hazardous food was not cleaned every four hours while in use or as frequently as required. -Tongs at hot hold burgers are changed daily. -Blizzard blender is rinsed with hot water and sanitized after each use. *Ensure full wash, rinse, and sanitize every fours or less while in use.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Residue at can opener blade and surfaces. -Tomato slicer was left dirty after use, food debris dried to blades and surfaces.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Debris in bottom of clean storage utensil bin at cake station. -Mild debris/splatter at dessert station container and pump exteriors. -Heavy spill debris in bottom of right side ice cream machine. -Flaky residue at gaskets of left side ice cream machine door.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Grease and heavy dust debris build up at hood surround surfaces. *Staff stated they are waiting for a slow day and lids to cover fryers.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -Ceiling tiles throughout kitchen are stained and/or acoustic ceiling tiles. -Door transition around walk in freezer is unfinished with exposed insulation.
